back_translation: And Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses concerning the Cushite woman whom he had taken; for he had taken a Cushite wife.
And they said: 'Only through Moses has the LORD spoken? Has He not spoken also through us?' And the LORD heard.
And the man Moses was very humble, more than all the people who were on the face of the earth.
And the LORD spoke suddenly to Moses and to Aaron and to Miriam: 'You three, come out to the Tent of Meeting.' And the three of them came out.
And the LORD came down in a pillar of cloud, and stood at the entrance of the Tent, and called Aaron and Miriam; and both of them came out.
And He said: 'Hear now My words: if there is a prophet among you, I, the LORD, will make Myself known to him in a vision; in a dream I will speak in him.'
Not so My servant Moses; in all My house he is faithful.
Mouth to mouth I speak in him, and visibly, and not in riddles; and the form of the LORD he beholds. And why were you not afraid to speak against My servant, against Moses?
And the anger of the LORD burned against them, and He departed.
And the cloud departed from over the Tent, and behold, Miriam was leprous like snow; and Aaron turned to Miriam, and behold, she was leprous.
And Aaron said to Moses: 'O my lord, please do not lay upon us sin which we have done foolishly, and which we have sinned.'
Let her not be like the dead, whose flesh is half consumed when he comes out from his mother's womb.
And Moses cried out to the LORD, saying: 'O God, please heal her, please.'

And the LORD said to Moses: 'If her father had only spit in her face, would she not be ashamed for seven days? Let her be shut up seven days outside the camp, and after that she shall be brought back.'
And Miriam was shut up outside the camp seven days; and the people did not set out until Miriam was brought back.
And after that the people set out from Hazeroth, and camped in the wilderness of Paran.
